Build Trust With ESG Assurance

What is ESG Assurance?

ESG Assurance is an independent verification process that confirms the accuracy, credibility, and completeness of your sustainability disclosures, whether it’s your Greenhouse Gas (GHG) inventory, Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR), or Annual Sustainability Report (ASR).

Using globally recognized standards like the AA1000 Standard (AA1000AS v3), third-party validates your data and builds stakeholder confidence. It also prepares your organization for regulatory scrutiny, investor evaluation, and global sustainability rankings. At Growlity, we offer independent assurance services tailored to your sustainability reporting needs, delivering credibility, transparency, and audit readiness across your journey.

Eligibility Check

We assess whether your products fall under CBAM-covered goods and evaluate your supply chain for risks.

Eligibility Check

Identify if your goods (steel, aluminum, cement, etc.) fall under scope.

Supply Chain Risk Scan

Evaluate suppliers for exposure and reporting responsibilities.

Compliance Readiness Guide

Get guidance on emission data and quarterly reporting needs.

Understanding

CBAM Timeline: Key Milestones

Transitional Phase

Definitive Phase

Transitional Phase

If your business exports to the EU in any of these sectors, CBAM applies to you.

  • Period: Oct 2023 – Dec 2025
  • Requirement: Quarterly reporting without financial payment

![CBAM report](https://growlity @media (max-width: 767px) { } /* 2. Inner Flex Layout & Gap */ /* 3. Button Wrapper Alignment */ /* 4. Button Mobile Padding */ @media (max-width: 767px) { } /* 5. Section Title Typography */

CBAM Report

Your report must include:

Type of product and country of origin

Production process and input materials

Embedded GHG emissions (Scope 1 & 2) per ton

Verification method and calculation approach

Supporting documents (LCA, emission factors, energy use)

Cover Every Detail in Reports

FAQs

On The Side of Ambitious

 Action 

We help brands stand out and grow by creating innovative, results-driven strategies that engage customers and drive success.

1. What is the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism?

It is an EU regulation that imposes a carbon cost on imports of specific carbon-intensive goods to align them with the EU’s climate ambitions and prevent carbon leakage.

2. When does CBAM become fully effective?

It is currently in its transitional phase (2023–2025). From 2026 onwards, importers will pay for carbon certificates based on reported embedded emissions.

3. How is the CBAM report submitted?

Reports are submitted quarterly on the EU portal, including emissions per product and country of origin. Non-compliance can result in penalties.

4. What is the cost or carbon tax under CBAM?

From 2026, importers must purchase carbon certificates priced according to the EU ETS carbon price, which currently fluctuates around €80–€100/ton CO₂e.

5. Can Growlity help prepare our documentation?

Yes. We offer full support – from emissions calculation, data validation, and documentation, to submission via the EU system.

Explore More

You might also Be Interested In…!!!

ESG Reporting

ESG Reporting refers to the structured disclosure of a company’s Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) performance. It enables businesses to demonstrate how they manage risks and opportunities across climate change, resource efficiency, human rights, ethical governance, diversity, and more. In today’s investor-driven, regulation-focused environment, ESG reporting is no longer optional—it’s a strategic imperative. ESG disclosures influence investment decisions, consumer trust, regulatory compliance, and corporate reputation.

Read More

ESG Reporting

Net Zero & Decarbonisation

Net Zero is no longer a distant ambition, it’s a business-critical commitment. At Growlity, we help organizations chart their Net Zero transformation with robust GHG accounting, practical decarbonisation strategies, and alignment with frameworks like the GHG Protocol, ISO 14064, SBTi, and CDP. Whether you’re just beginning your decarbonisation journey or already setting net zero targets, our sustainability consultants deliver tailored solutions that are strategic, science-based, and results-driven.

Read More

Net Zero & Decarbonisation

CDP Reporting

CDP is a globally recognized environmental disclosure system that enables organizations to report their carbon emissions, climate-related risks, and environmental performance. Trusted by thousands of investors, companies, and cities, CDP plays a critical role in driving climate transparency and accountability. As a CDP consultant and disclosure partner, Growlity empowers organizations to navigate the full CDP reporting framework, enhance data quality, and improve scores. Our expert support ensures climate compliance, strengthens sustainability disclosures, and reinforces stakeholder trust—positioning your business for credible, low-carbon growth.

Read More

CDP Reporting
